mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already above 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which sustains corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term shows negative alpha in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, causing short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of contraction episodes, or the signal shows no statistical significance during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below the core inflation rate, real borrowing costs turn negative, encouraging equity risk-taking and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The negative real rate regime produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the spread loses predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms strong economic momentum, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week of data release.
failure_condition: High GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the signal shows no edge after adjusting for earnings seasonality.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment indicates healthy capacity utilization without labor market overheating, supporting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases while PAYEMS growth stays between 0.5% and 1.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of triggered months, or the mechanism breaks down when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
